title: The Eternal Skald
description:
An AI-powered storyteller, oracle interpreter, and tactical enemy controller for Ironsworn and Ironsworn: Delve campaigns in Foundry VTT v14, powered by Abacus AI ChatLLM. Just type ! followed by your words to speak with the Skald, or !skald-help for all commands.

v0.25.6 — Settings menu tidy: the tabbed settings panel now sorts every visible option into a logical tab — text-to-speech and AI-capability toggles under Narrative, and all semantic-memory, RAG and AI-context options under Memory — so nothing falls through to a catch-all Advanced list. Purely organisational and fully backwards-compatible. Builds on v0.25.5 — Narration respects progress: AI narration now honours the mechanical progress-track state — post-roll narration is rewired to receive the valid-move whitelist and journey-pacing rules, completion moves ("Fulfill Your Vow", "End the Fight", "Reach Your Destination") only become clickable rolls when their track is fully charted (10/10), and the exact-10/10 completion gate is now symmetric across journeys, vows and fights (intentionally stricter than Ironsworn RAW). Builds on v0.25.4 — Journey lifecycle & pacing: a fully-charted journey (10/10) is no longer auto-completed — the progress track now stays OPEN so the player rolls "Reach Your Destination" to resolve HOW the arrival goes; after each narration an open journey deterministically offers the correct next progress move; and inline move links capture the surrounding narration so journeys auto-name from a real destination. Builds on v0.25.3 — Settings UI fix: module settings now register on the i18nInit hook so their labels resolve to real text instead of raw localization keys, and the tabbed settings panel uses a flex layout that keeps the "Save Changes" button visible on every screen size. Builds on v0.25.2 — Journey tracking fix (cont.): "Undertake a Journey" now advances on the actor that actually rolled (not the selected token) and adopts an existing generic journey instead of spawning a duplicate, with always-on diagnostics for the reuse-vs-create decision. Builds on v0.25.1 — Journey tracking fix: "Undertake a Journey" reliably advances the existing journey instead of spawning duplicate progress tracks. Builds on v0.25.0 — Narration & story memory. Optionally embed the unfolding story into semantic memory: AI-generated Skald story cards and player in-character (IC/EMOTE) narration are indexed for recall, while out-of-character chat, dice rolls, system messages, commands and whispers are categorically excluded. Story-only by design, opt-in, debounced and micro-batched so embedding never blocks chat. Builds on v0.24.0 — Selectable memory models: GM-selectable embedding-model architecture for semantic memory (MiniLM default, BGE-small, GTE-small, 768-dim Nomic Embed) with a one-click confirm-and-reindex switch — additive and backwards-compatible. Builds on the opt-in HNSW approximate-nearest-neighbour index for semantic memory and Phase E: an AI tool-calling architecture, ruleset-aware narration for Starforged & Sundered Isles, read-only system adapters for D&D 5e and Pathfinder 2e, optional browser-native text-to-speech narration, and fail-soft integrations with Simple Calendar, Monk's Enhanced Journals and Dice So Nice. Every addition is opt-in / default-safe and fully backwards-compatible. Full release history is in CHANGELOG.md.

recommends.reason: Optional but recommended. When the Ironsworn system is active, the Skald integrates with its rules engine to trigger moves, read character state, and narrate official roll outcomes. The module also works standalone in any system, but Ironsworn-specific mechanics are then unavailable.

Optional. When the Nimble system is active, the Skald reads character abilities (STR/DEX/INT/WIL) and resource pools (HP, wounds, mana, hit dice) through a built-in read-only system adapter, so narration is grounded in your real sheet. Map vision and the agnostic chronicle/memory core also work. Nimble support is read-only — the Skald does not yet write to Nimble sheets.
